title: Sci-Fi
subtitle: Cerebral sci-fi films, prioritized by fit with your taste signal.

sections.name: I. High Priority
sections.desc: Strongest probable fits. Calibrated to: 2001 loved, Timecrimes loved, Contact loved, character-wins-ties, smart-structure-loving.

items.title: Coherence
items.whyPriority: Direct Timecrimes/Primer descendant. Eight friends, one house, reality forks during a comet flyby. Smart structure + character-driven + improvised performances. Highest-confidence pick.
items.pitch: Eight friends at a dinner party during a comet flyby. Reality starts forking. Shot for ~$50k in a single house, mostly improvised. The closest cousin to Primer's puzzle-box quality.

critics.quote: Won multiple festival awards. Built reputation through word of mouth.
Cult sensation. Critics including Mike D'Angelo praised the structural rigor of the screenplay.

items.title: Predestination
items.whyPriority: Heinlein paradox film. Tight structure that explains its rules then lets you discover the implications. Surprisingly emotional. Best to go in cold.
items.pitch: Time-travel paradox film based on a Heinlein short story. Tight, twisty, surprisingly emotional.

critics.quote: Strong reviews from genre press. Mark Kermode positive. Underseen on release.

items.title: Synchronicity
items.whyPriority: Added based on your Timecrimes love. Low-budget time-loop noir, physicist meets future self. Blade Runner aesthetic with the structural rigor you actually want.
items.pitch: Low-budget time-loop noir. A physicist's experiment crosses paths with his future self. Unmistakable Primer/Timecrimes lineage with neon-soaked Blade Runner aesthetics.

critics.quote: Mixed reviews — some critics found the Blade Runner homage too on-the-nose. Genre-press champions appreciated the structural discipline.

items.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
items.whyPriority: Charlie Kaufman script. Memory-erasure premise that's character-first, structure-tight, emotionally devastating. The Contact-warmth + Nolan-structure intersection.
items.pitch: After a brutal breakup, a couple separately undergoes a memory-erasure procedure. Then they meet again. Kaufman's structure is fragmented but always coherent.

critics.quote: Highly positive. Called Kaufman's screenplay 'a marvel of construction.'
Won Best Original Screenplay (Kaufman, Gondry, Bismuth).

items.title: 12 Monkeys
items.whyPriority: Direct adaptation of La Jetée's premise into a feature. Time-travel, paradox, emotional weight. Pairs naturally with watching La Jetée first.
items.pitch: A convict from a plague-ravaged future is sent back to gather information. Brad Pitt's breakthrough. Built directly on La Jetée's structure but expanded into a full character-driven thriller.

critics.quote: Three and a half stars. Praised Gilliam's 'committed lunacy.'
Best Supporting Actor nomination — rare for a sci-fi film.

items.title: Gattaca
items.whyPriority: Genetic-determinism premise, character-first, rules cleanly explained. The 'character wins ties' film par excellence in 90s sci-fi. Aged unusually well.
items.pitch: Near-future where genetic engineering determines class. A naturally-born man assumes the identity of a genetically-enhanced one to pursue space travel. Patient, cool, melancholy.

critics.quote: Three and a half stars. Praised the 'cool, intellectual passion' of the filmmaking.
Routinely cited by working scientists as one of the most thoughtful Hollywood films about genetics ever made.

sections.name: II. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Likely to land but with one or more variables. Watch when high-priority queue is exhausted.

items.title: La Jetée
items.whyPriority: 28 minutes. Watch before 12 Monkeys. Photo-roman structure may either fascinate or frustrate — short enough that the risk is minimal.
items.pitch: A 28-minute photo-roman about memory, time-travel, and a man haunted by an image from before the apocalypse. The film 12 Monkeys was based on.

critics.quote: Initially obscure, championed by Cahiers du Cinéma; entered the global canon through Sight & Sound polls.

items.title: Primer
items.pitch: Two engineers accidentally invent time travel and discover the universe doesn't forgive paradoxes. Made for $7,000. Demanding.

critics.quote: Won the Grand Jury Prize.

items.title: Solaris (Tarkovsky)
items.whyPriority: The arthouse foundation. Pace may test patience but the emotional core is exactly your wheelhouse — grief, memory, love. Watch before Soderbergh's.
items.pitch: Psychologist sent to a space station orbiting a sentient ocean that manifests his dead wife. Glacial, devastating.

critics.quote: Won the Grand Jury Prize.
'A film of substance and ambition.'

items.title: Solaris (Soderbergh)
items.whyPriority: Tighter, warmer, more character-forward than Tarkovsky's. 99 min vs. 166. Most accessible version of the story.
items.pitch: Soderbergh's distillation. Cleaner emotional arc, Clooney genuinely good, score by Cliff Martinez.

critics.quote: Four stars. Called it 'a film of great visual beauty and considerable depth.'

items.title: Moon
items.whyPriority: Sam Rockwell, lunar isolation, identity puzzle. Character-first, smart structure. Likely Loved territory.
items.pitch: A solo helium-3 miner near the end of his three-year contract begins to suspect things aren't what they seem.

critics.quote: Premiered to widespread acclaim. Sam Rockwell's performance widely seen as career-best.

items.title: Dark City
items.whyPriority: Ebert called it the best film of 1998. Memory/identity puzzle with answers that resolve cleanly.
items.pitch: A man wakes in a city where it's always night and the buildings rearrange while everyone sleeps.

critics.quote: Named it the best film of 1998. Wrote a Great Movies essay on it.

items.title: Pi
items.pitch: A mathematician convinces himself he's found a number that explains everything. Black-and-white, paranoid, low-fi.

critics.quote: Won the Directing Award.

items.title: The Andromeda Strain
items.whyPriority: Crichton procedural, scientifically grounded. Closest to Contact in temperament — adults solving an extraterrestrial problem.
items.pitch: Scientists in an underground lab race to identify an extraterrestrial pathogen. Almost no action; protocol, hypothesis, dread.

critics.quote: Praised for its restraint and 'cool intelligence.'

items.title: Cube
items.pitch: Strangers wake in a deadly geometric prison. Microbudget Canadian. Lean, philosophical, mean.

critics.quote: Won Best Canadian First Feature.

items.title: Children of Men
items.whyPriority: Religious-political collapse. Direct fit for your Dune political/religious signal. Long-take cinematography is legendary.
items.pitch: Near-future Britain where humanity has become infertile. Bleakness in service of hope.

critics.quote: Highly positive. Called the cinematography 'astonishing.'
Listed it among the decade's best films.

sections.name: III. Standard
sections.desc: Foundational films with moderate confidence in fit.

items.title: Donnie Darko
items.pitch: Suburban teenager has visions of a six-foot rabbit predicting the end of the world. Watch the theatrical cut first.

critics.quote: Built reputation slowly through DVD. Now considered a defining film of millennial sci-fi.

items.title: The Man Who Fell to Earth
items.pitch: David Bowie as an alien who comes to Earth for water and gets corrupted by capitalism, addiction, and television.

critics.quote: Originally 2.5 stars; reissued at 3 stars in 2011.

items.title: Stalker
items.pitch: Three men journey into a forbidden Zone where physics is unreliable.

critics.quote: S&S Greatest Films poll fixture since the 1990s.

items.title: Silent Running
items.pitch: Bruce Dern alone on a spaceship tending Earth's last forests. Ecological, sad, quiet.

critics.quote: Three stars. Praised the 'gentle, melancholy spirit.'

items.title: Phase IV
items.pitch: Saul Bass's only feature. Ants in the Arizona desert achieve collective intelligence.

critics.quote: The discovery of the cut original ending in 2012 transformed its critical standing.

items.title: Ex Machina
items.pitch: Three characters, one location, AI Turing-test thriller.

critics.quote: Highly positive. 'A sleek and accessible work of speculative fiction.'

items.title: Annihilation
items.pitch: Five women enter 'The Shimmer,' a zone where biology rewrites itself.

critics.quote: Strongly positive.

items.title: After Yang
items.pitch: A family's android caretaker breaks down. Quiet, contemplative.

critics.quote: 'A film of patient, accumulating beauty.'

items.title: Open Your Eyes
items.pitch: The original that Vanilla Sky remade. Sharper, colder than the remake.

critics.quote: Established Amenábar's career.

items.title: Sleep Dealer
items.pitch: Near-future Mexico cyberpunk rooted in immigration politics.

critics.quote: Won the Waldo Salt Screenwriting Award.

items.title: Europa Report
items.pitch: Found-footage mission to Jupiter's moon. Genuinely tries to be scientifically grounded.

critics.quote: Praised by NASA scientists for fidelity.

items.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
items.pitch: Listed for completeness — already loved.

items.title: Blade Runner
items.pitch: Listed for completeness.

items.title: Timecrimes
items.pitch: Listed for completeness — already loved.

items.title: Dune
items.pitch: Lynch's polarizing adaptation. You loved it.

items.title: Dune: Part One
items.pitch: Villeneuve's first half. You loved it.

items.title: Dune: Part Two
items.pitch: Villeneuve's second half. You loved it.

sections.name: V. Lower Priority — Watch With Caution
sections.desc: Quality films, but your signal suggests they may not land.

items.title: Beyond the Black Rainbow
items.pitch: More tone poem than narrative — flagged for 'style over substance' risk.

items.title: Upstream Color
items.pitch: Carruth's follow-up to Primer. More elliptical — like Arrival's emotional approach. Mixed signal for you.

items.title: High Life
items.pitch: Cold Denis-style. Low fit given character-first preference.

items.title: Possessor
items.pitch: Body horror sci-fi, very bleak. At edge of tolerance.

items.title: Hard to Be a God
items.pitch: Three hours of medieval squalor. Lower priority given character-first signal.

items.title: Possession
items.pitch: Cold War Berlin marriage horror. At edge of tolerance.

items.title: Altered States
items.pitch: Maximalist body horror with a brain.

items.title: Brazil
items.pitch: Bureaucratic dystopia, dream-logic.

items.title: Sunshine
items.pitch: Mission to reignite the dying sun. Third act controversial.

items.title: Triangle
items.pitch: Yacht caught in a storm; loop begins. Same structural rigor as Timecrimes.

items.title: The Platform
items.pitch: Vertical prison. Bleak.

sections.name: VI. Adjacent — Sci-Fi-Tinged
sections.desc: On the edge of the genre. Each tagged so you can decide.

items.title: Seconds
items.whyPriority: Identity-replacement thriller, character-first, paranoid. Strong fit despite being adjacent.
items.pitch: Middle-aged businessman pays a shadowy company for a new identity.

critics.quote: Booed at Cannes. Critically rehabilitated within a decade.

items.title: The Conversation
items.whyPriority: Surveillance/tech-anxiety thriller. Hackman career-best. Palme d'Or.
items.pitch: Surveillance expert listens to a recording that may capture a murder plot.

critics.quote: Palme d'Or.

items.title: Strange Days
items.pitch: Y2K-era LA. People trade illegal recordings of others' memories.

sections.name: VII. Auteur Filmographies
sections.desc: Primary genre home for director-specific sci-fi works appearing in the Auteur tab.

items.title: Solaris
items.pitch: A psychologist is sent to a space station orbiting an ocean planet that materializes the crew's memories and guilt. Tarkovsky's answer to 2001.

items.title: Arrival
items.whyPriority: The film that proved Villeneuve could do thoughtful sci-fi. Adams's career-best.
items.pitch: A linguist is recruited to communicate with newly arrived alien spacecraft.

critics.quote: Eight Oscar nominations including Best Picture, Best Director.

items.title: Blade Runner 2049
items.whyPriority: Sequel to a film you've watched. Roger Deakins's Oscar-winning cinematography. Slower, more contemplative than the original.
items.pitch: Decades after the original. A blade runner uncovers a secret with implications for the entire society.

critics.quote: Won 2 Oscars including Best Cinematography (Deakins). Five total nominations.
